strengths: Direct AI agent development experience: current re:cinq role explicitly covers MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure — a rare, high-weight requirement.

20+ years of polyglot fullstack depth with recurring TypeScript/Node.js/React across multiple senior and staff-level positions, including high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s).
Deeply embedded testing culture: created Fluent-Asserts (TDD library), organized Refactoring Dojos, delivered multi-module TDD/Selenium courses, and applied TDD/CI/CD/trunk-based dev at RIDE Capital.
concerns: Nest.js is entirely absent from the resume — it is the primary backend framework listed, and no equivalent structured framework experience is named.
Specific testing frameworks (Vitest, Jest, Playwright) are never mentioned by name; TDD practice is evident but alignment with the exact required toolchain is unverified.
Kotlin is not mentioned anywhere; Java appears only in embedded/robotics contexts at Altom, leaving the secondary backend stack (Kotlin/Java for services) weakly covered.
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong match for the core of this role. His current position at re:cinq directly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — a differentiating requirement few candidates will satisfy. Fullstack TypeScript/Node.js/React experience is present across multiple senior roles, including a high-throughput production system, and his testing culture is demonstrably deep. The primary gap is Nest.js: it is the stated backend framework and does not appear anywhere in the resume, which introduces ramp-up risk on day one. Kotlin is similarly absent. These are learnable gaps for a developer of his caliber, but they are real. PostgreSQL is not explicitly cited (MongoDB and MySQL are). On balance, the AI-agent fit and fullstack seniority outweigh the framework gaps.
